title: Release runbook — vennDiagramLab (R)
text: Bioconductor submission goes public issue tracker. Bioc Single Package Builder (SPB) auto-checks package across platforms + Bioc dependencies. URL: https://github.com/Bioconductor/Contributions/issues/new Title: vennDiagramLab Body template: AdditionalPackage: URL must end repository root (/r). Bioc SPB handles r/ subdir via bioc.subdir field package’s BiocManager metadata. Within minutes: SPB bot leaves comment build status across Bioc-supported R versions platforms. First-pass typical issues: SPB may flag missing biocViews terms, missing methods documentation, version-mismatch Bioc devel branch. Address code + push main; SPB re-checks automatically. Manual review (4-8 weeks): Bioc team member + community reviewers read package + ask questions issue thread. acceptance: package added next Bioconductor release branch (releases happen every 6 months, April + October). Initial Bioc version: Bioc convention 0.99.X first submission (becomes 1.0.X upon release). spec keeps 2.0.0 match unified monorepo version. reviewer may request 0.99.X version specifically — , bump r/DESCRIPTION::Version 0.99.0, push, re-trigger SPB. CRAN submission Bioc submission can run parallel — ’re independent registries.

title: Release runbook — vennDiagramLab (R)
text: CRAN URL check — every URL DESCRIPTION + man pages + vignettes HTTP-HEAD’d. Broken URLs become NOTEs. worked around Phase 8 pointing README GitHub tree (gh-pages yet live); Phase 9 reverts gh-pages deployed. CRAN policy: examples must run <5s — \donttest{} examples exempt CRAN runs R CMD check (--run-donttest). r-cmd-check.yml CI cells run --run-donttest catch real failures. Bioc reviewer style preferences — Bioc reviewers sometimes prefer vapply sapply, seq_len(n) 1:n, BiocStyle vignettes rmarkdown::html_vignette. use latter; acceptable v2.0.0 release. Zenodo DOI ordering — parent (concept) DOI stays across versions; version-specific DOIs change. Cite parent DOI CITATION.cff “software” version-specific DOI release notes.

title: Real cancer drivers walkthrough
text: vignette uses bundled dataset_real_cancer_drivers_4 dataset illustrate real biological analysis: four canonical cancer driver catalogs overlap? four sources : Vogelstein — 138-gene catalog Vogelstein et al. (Science 2013), often cited “core” oncogene set. COSMIC_CGC — COSMIC Cancer Gene Census (Sondka et al. 2018), curated list genes causally implicated cancer. OncoKB — MSK precision-oncology knowledge base annotation level ≥ “Oncogenic” (Chakravarty et al. 2017). IntOGen — pan-cancer driver mutations IntOGen pipeline (Martínez-Jiménez et al. 2020).

title: Statistics deep dive (Jaccard, Dice, hypergeometric, BH-FDR)
text: vennDiagramLab reports five pairwise metrics every set pair plus multiple-testing correction. vignette explains metric means, prefer , reproduce values appear web tool’s significance coloring.

title: Statistics deep dive (Jaccard, Dice, hypergeometric, BH-FDR)
text: two sets B sizes ||, |B| intersection |∩ B| drawn universe N items: Jaccard = |∩ B| / |∪ B|. Range [0, 1]. Symmetric. Dice = 2 |∩ B| / (|| + |B|). Range [0, 1]. Symmetric. Always >= Jaccard; two relate Dice = 2J / (1 + J). Overlap coefficient = |∩ B| / min(||, |B|). Range [0, 1]. Equal 1 one set contained . Hypergeometric p-value — probability observing |∩ B| shared items chance, given ||, |B|, N. Tests - representation. Fold enrichment = (|∩ B| * N) / (|| * |B|). ratio observed expected intersection size independence. > 1 - representation.

title: Statistics deep dive (Jaccard, Dice, hypergeometric, BH-FDR)
text: helpers exported stateless: hypergeometric p-value essentially zero: 100 shared genes expected (138 * 581) / 20000 ≈ 4 25× enrichment.

title: Statistics deep dive (Jaccard, Dice, hypergeometric, BH-FDR)
text: stats@hypergeometric already carries BH-FDR-adjusted q-value (p_adjusted) boolean significant (q < 0.05) highly_significant (q < 0.001). adjustment uses stats::p.adjust(method = "BH"): unrelated p-values, BH-FDR permissive Bonferroni conservative correction:
code: raw_p <- stats@hypergeometric$p_value adjusted <- bh_fdr(raw_p) all.equal(adjusted, stats@hypergeometric$p_adjusted) #> [1] TRUE toy_p <- c(0.001, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.5, 0.9) data.frame(     raw            = toy_p,     bonferroni     = pmin(toy_p * length(toy_p), 1),     bh_fdr         = bh_fdr(toy_p) ) #>     raw bonferroni     bh_fdr #> 1 0.001      0.007 0.00700000 #> 2 0.005      0.035 0.01750000 #> 3 0.010      0.070 0.02333333 #> 4 0.050      0.350 0.08750000 #> 5 0.100      0.700 0.14000000 #> 6 0.500      1.000 0.58333333 #> 7 0.900      1.000 0.90000000

title: Headless Venn Diagram Analysis and Rendering
text: 44 SVG diagram models (2- 9-set) dozen construction methods (Venn 1880, Edwards 1996, Anderson 1988, Grünbaum 1984/1992, Bannier-Bodin 2017, Carroll 2000, Mamakani 2012, SUMO-Venn) — library shipped web tool. Area-proportional 2- 3-set layouts via analytical (solve_2set) approximate (solve_3set) solvers. UpSet plots via ComplexUpset sort--size / sort--degree, depth / heatmap / custom color modes, threshold cutoffs. Force-directed network views via ggraph + tidygraph, configurable edge metric (intersection / Jaccard / fold enrichment / overlap coefficient) significance coloring. Five pairwise statistical metrics (Jaccard, Dice, overlap coefficient, fold enrichment, hypergeometric -representation) BH-FDR adjustment. Multi-page PDF reports combining overview, Venn + UpSet, statistics tables, network, methodology pages single US-Letter-landscape document. ggplot2 layer (geom_venn()) broom-compatible S3 methods (tidy() / glance() / augment()) tidyverse + targets / drake pipeline integration. Byte-equivalent TSV exports tested React webapp’s golden fixtures — region-summary, item-matrix, statistics files web tool’s “Export” buttons emit. Cross-implementation parity verified 12 byte-equivalence tests Python package’s golden fixtures (4 sample datasets × 3 export types).

title: VennDataset: in-memory representation of a Venn-diagram input — VennDataset-class
text: set_names Ordered character vector set identifiers (length 2-9). items Named list (`names(items) == set_names`) character vectors, containing deduplicated members corresponding set. item_order First-seen insertion order items across sets, matching JS Set/Map semantics. Used TSV writers (Phase 2) byte-equivalent output web tool. universe_size Hypergeometric universe N (population size) source file, known. Binary CSV/TSV loaders set row count (matching web tool's `csv.rows.length`); formats leave `NULL`, signaling "compute length(item_order)" downstream. source_path Original file path loaded disk; `NULL` -memory datasets. format Source format: one `"csv"`, `"tsv"`, `"gmt"`, `"gmx"`.

title: Parse a Boolean region expression into bitmasks — parse_region_expression
text: Grammar: ``, `B`, ..., `` — set atoms (uppercase ASCII). `&` — intersection. `|` `+` — union. `~` `!` — complement (unary, `1..2^n_sets - 1`). `(`, `)` — grouping. Precedence (highest first): `~ !`, `&`, `| +`. Binary operators left-associative.

title: Parse a Boolean region expression into bitmasks — parse_region_expression

text: Loads bundled SVG template `result@model` (explicit `model` override), walks DOM via xml2 overwrite text content (`Name*`, `Count_*`, `CountSUM_*`, `Title`) inline `fill:` colors (`Shape*`, `Shape*2` Euler extras, `Bullet*`), serializes back string.

code: render_venn_svg(   result,   model = NULL,   set_names = NULL,   colors = NULL,   title = NULL,   show_names = TRUE,   show_counts = TRUE,   show_items = FALSE,   item_options = NULL,   highlight = NULL )

title: Render a RegionResult onto its model SVG and return the raw SVG string — render_venn_svg
text: result [`RegionResult-class`]. model Optional model id override (filename stem). Default = `result@model`. set_names Optional named character vector mapping letters (`""`, `"B"`, ...) display names. Unspecified letters fall back `result@dataset@set_names`. colors Optional named character vector mapping letters fill hex colors. Applies `BulletX`, `ShapeX`, `ShapeX2` (Euler extra shapes). title Optional title override. `NULL`, template's default title text preserved. show_names `FALSE`, blanks every `NameA-` element. show_counts `FALSE`, blanks every `Count_*` `CountSUM_*` element. show_items `TRUE`, replace per-region count text actual item identifiers (rendered `<tspan>` lines inside `Count_*` text node). Default `FALSE`. item_options Named list overrides item-text engine. Recognised keys: `max_items_per_region` (default 20), `ncol_items` (default 1), `truncate_long_names` (default 12; 0 disables), `line_height` (default 10), `font_size` (default 8), `show_counts_with_items` (default `FALSE`), `ellipsis` (default `"..."`). Unknown keys raise warning. highlight Character vector region labels (e.g. `c("AB", "ABC")`) integer vector region bitmasks (e.g. output [parse_region_expression()]). set, listed regions keep original fill colour; set-shapes desaturated `#cccccc` 25% opacity. Default `NULL` (spotlight).

title: Write the pairwise Statistics TSV — to_statistics_tsv
text: Columns: Set_A, Set_B, Name_A, Name_B, Size_A, Size_B, Intersection, Union, Jaccard, Overlap_Coeff, Dice, Expected, Fold_Enrichment, P_value, FDR, Significant. Float formatting: * Jaccard / Overlap_Coeff / Dice: 4 decimals via [.js_to_fixed()] * Expected: 2 decimals * Fold_Enrichment: 3 decimals * P_value / FDR: scientific (JS toExponential(2)) `< 0.001`, else 6 decimals * Significant: one `"***"`, `"**"`, `"*"`, `"ns"` keyed FDR thresholds   (0.001, 0.01, 0.05). Rows sorted P_value ascending (matches underlying StatisticsResult).
